Friday, May 8, 2026 Daily Digest

HubSpot Launches AEO for AI Visibility, Meta's "Hatch" Agents Go Autonomous, RPA Evolves to AI-Driven Outcomes

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

HubSpot Launches AEO — "Answer Engine Optimization" for the AI Era

At Spring Spotlight, HubSpot unveiled HubSpot AEO — a tool that lets marketers see how their brand appears in AI assistants like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Perplexity, then take actions to improve visibility. This is the clearest signal yet that "AI visibility" is becoming a core marketing discipline. If your brand isn't optimized for AI answer engines, you're invisible to a growing segment of buyers.

Read on Motley Fool →
2

Meta's "Hatch" AI Agents Can Now Act Autonomously

Meta is rolling out agentic AI tools under the "Hatch" brand that can perform tasks on users' behalf with minimal input — from summarizing messages to creating entire ad campaigns. The shift from "AI that assists" to "AI that acts" is accelerating, and Meta is betting advertisers want agents that execute, not just recommend.

Read on Optimixed →
3

Iterable's Nova Agent Automates Marketing Journeys

Iterable introduced Nova Agent to automatically create and optimize marketing journeys. The tool analyzes customer data to decide which message to send next in a sequence — removing the manual work of building workflows. Customer engagement platforms are racing to add agent-style features, and the "set it and forget it" promise is finally becoming real.

Read on MarTech →
4

RPA Is Evolving from Task Automation to AI-Driven Outcomes

ConnectWise highlights how robotic process automation is moving beyond simple task execution (updating ticket statuses, data entry) to AI-driven outcomes that can make decisions and adapt. For enterprise teams drowning in manual processes, this evolution means automation that actually thinks — not just follows scripts.

Read on Industry Analysts →
5

Business Leaders Embrace Human-Machine "Power Partnerships"

New research shows the most effective AI teams aren't replacing humans — they're creating "power partnerships" where AI handles data patterns and humans handle strategy and creativity. IBM argues AI is most useful when it connects data across channels and identifies patterns that inform action. The winning formula: human judgment + machine scale.

Read on Noah News →

💡 My Take

The HubSpot AEO launch is the story. "Answer Engine Optimization" is now a thing with a dedicated product from a major platform. If you're not thinking about how your brand shows up when buyers ask ChatGPT or Perplexity questions, you're already behind. This is the new SEO — and the land grab is happening now.
